narrative.headline: 2018 Honda Pilot: engine complaints lead the list
narrative.summary: Owners filed 423 complaints with NHTSA. The engine is the most reported area by a wide margin at 35%, followed by the electrical system (19%) and the power train (18%). The fuel system and gasoline each account for 15%. No deaths are reported, and serious outcomes are rare: just 1 fire (engine), 1 crash (electrical system), and 1 injury (power train) across all reports.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: The single biggest complaint: 148 reports (35%), including 1 with a fire. No crashes or injuries reported. The most common area to check.

issues.sample: VEHICLE WAS STOPPED IN A PARKING LOT IN "DRIVE" WITH THE AUTO-IDLE FEATURE IN USE. CAR WOULD NOT RE-START WHEN FOOT TAKEN OFF BRAKE. RADIO CUT OUT BRIEFLY AS WELL. DASHBOARD LIT UP WITH MANY ERROR MESSAGES - ONE OF THEM WAS "COLLISION MITIGATION PROBLEM" OR SOMETHING SIMILAR. PLA
issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: Second most reported: 82 reports (19%), with 1 crash. No fires or injuries reported. Watch for warning messages and electrical glitches.

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: 77 reports (18%), tied to 1 injury. Feel for jerking, hesitation, or clunking when the transmission shifts gears on a test drive.

issues.title: Fuel System
issues.blurb: 62 reports (15%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries reported. Several owners describe losing power while driving.
